title: Llama7B mixed/int8 score32 generation quality
hypothesis: Because the score32 mixed/int8 top1 misses were narrow-margin and top-k stable, a bounded native-checkpoint generation/NLL gate can determine whether the remaining drift is acceptable enough to justify score32 or exact-softmax PPA recosting.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does the score32 mixed/int8 attention path preserve bounded greedy generation and teacher-forced reference-token likelihood on a 7B checkpoint?
include: run native-checkpoint reference and score32 mixed/int8 candidate on the same prompt set
measure teacher-forced reference-token NLL under reference and candidate
measure free-running greedy token match and first divergence step
record generation/NLL quality before any new exact-softmax or score32 PPA recost
keep the job evidence-only and bounded by prompt/step defaults

expected_benefit: decide whether narrow-margin score32 drift can proceed to PPA recost
reduce quality abstraction beyond teacher-forced attention-shadow top1 metrics
avoid spending physical-design time on a candidate that changes generated text too often
risks: bounded greedy generation is still not full task accuracy or broad perplexity
prompt/step defaults may miss longer-horizon divergence

required_evaluations.objective: Run bounded native-checkpoint generation/NLL quality for score32 mixed/int8 before recosting PPA.

expected_result.reason: The result should determine whether score32 is quality-safe enough for exact/high-precision softmax physical recosting.
